terms.definition_en: A set of rules and protocols that allows different software applications to communicate with each other.
terms.definition_es: Conjunto de reglas y protocolos que permite que diferentes aplicaciones de software se comuniquen entre sí.
examples: A weather app uses a REST API to fetch temperature data.
GitHub provides an API to manage repositories programmatically.

terms.definition_en: A specific URL in an API where requests can be sent to access or manipulate a resource.
terms.definition_es: Una URL específica en una API donde se pueden enviar solicitudes para acceder o manipular un recurso.
examples: The endpoint /users/123 retrieves data for the user with ID 123.
POST /api/login is the endpoint for user authentication.

terms.definition_en: An architectural style for designing networked applications, using HTTP requests to access and manipulate data.
terms.definition_es: Un estilo arquitectónico para diseñar aplicaciones en red, utilizando solicitudes HTTP para acceder y manipular datos.
examples: A REST API uses GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE methods.
Most modern web services are built following REST principles.

terms.definition_en: A collection of tools, libraries, and documentation that developers use to build applications for a specific platform.
terms.definition_es: Una colección de herramientas, bibliotecas y documentación que los desarrolladores usan para construir aplicaciones para una plataforma específica.
examples: The Android SDK provides tools to build Android apps.
Firebase SDK lets you integrate Firebase services into your app.

terms.definition_en: The foundation protocol of data communication for the World Wide Web, defining how messages are formatted and transmitted.
terms.definition_es: El protocolo de comunicación de datos fundamental para la World Wide Web, que define cómo se formatean y transmiten los mensajes.
examples: When you type a URL in a browser, it sends an HTTP GET request.
HTTP status 404 means the requested resource was not found.

terms.definition_en: The part of a software application that users interact with directly, typically the visual interface rendered in a browser.
terms.definition_es: La parte de una aplicación de software con la que los usuarios interactúan directamente, típicamente la interfaz visual renderizada en un navegador.
examples: React and Vue.js are popular frontend frameworks.
The frontend handles user input, buttons, and visual design.

terms.definition_en: The server-side part of an application responsible for business logic, database interactions, and API handling.
terms.definition_es: La parte del lado del servidor de una aplicación responsable de la lógica de negocio, interacciones con bases de datos y manejo de APIs.
examples: Python with FastAPI is commonly used for backend development.
The backend validates data before storing it in the database.

terms.definition_en: A pre-built structure or set of tools that provides a foundation for developing software applications more efficiently.
terms.definition_es: Una estructura preconstruida o conjunto de herramientas que proporciona una base para desarrollar aplicaciones de software de manera más eficiente.
examples: React is a JavaScript framework for building user interfaces.
Django is a Python framework for web development.

terms.definition_en: An organized collection of structured data stored electronically, managed by a database management system (DBMS).
terms.definition_es: Una colección organizada de datos estructurados almacenados electrónicamente, gestionada por un sistema de gestión de bases de datos (DBMS).
examples: MySQL and PostgreSQL are relational databases.
MongoDB is a NoSQL database that stores data as documents.

terms.definition_en: A request for data or information from a database, typically written in SQL or a similar language.
terms.definition_es: Una solicitud de datos o información de una base de datos, típicamente escrita en SQL o un lenguaje similar.
examples: SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 18 is a SQL query.
A query can filter, sort, or aggregate data from a database.

categories.architecture: Patterns and structures for system design

inference_rules.description: If a user searches for a term, suggest all directly related terms
If a term has difficulty=beginner, prioritize showing it before intermediate ones
If two terms share the same category, they are contextually related
